commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add landing page, pagination, and consistent UI across all pages (#8)

- index.php: Replace redirect with a public landing page announcing
  "Utility App for Decantalize" with a login link to orders.php
- orders.php: Add cursor-based pagination (25/page, oldest-first);
  add navbar with brand; update title to follow "{page} - Utility App"
  pattern
- install.php: Check existing access token and test it via shop.json
  before starting OAuth flow; render proper HTML success pages for both
  token-already-working and post-OAuth-completion states; standardise
  page title and add shared navbar/card layout

All pages share a consistent dark-navy (#1a1a2e) navbar, card layout,
and button style derived from the original orders.php design.

commit.message:
feat(compiler): 🎉 add Task 0 toolchain and skeleton driver (#63)

## Summary

Establishes the buildable, testable, formatted C++23 project skeleton
for the Dao compiler (Implementation Plan Task 0). Introduces CMake +
Conan build infrastructure, a skeleton `daoc` driver, formatting/linting
config, and CI.

## Highlights

- **Build system**: CMake 3.30+ with Conan 2.x managing boost-ext/ut
2.3.1; CMake presets generated by Conan
- **Skeleton driver**: `compiler/driver/main.cpp` — `daoc <file>` reads
a source file and exits cleanly
- **Formatting/linting**: `.clang-format` (LLVM base, 2-space indent,
100-col, no bin packing, pointer-left, no short control statements) and
`.clang-tidy` (modernize/performance/readability)
- **Conan profile**: `profiles/clang-21-debug` with locked deps at
`locks/clang-21-debug.lock`
- **CI**: GitHub Actions pipeline — cmake build, daoc smoke test,
clang-format check, clang-tidy check
- **Tooling pin**: `mise.toml` pins Conan 2.26.2

## Test plan

- [ ] `conan install . --output-folder=build/debug --build=missing -pr
profiles/clang-21-debug`
- [ ] `cmake --preset conan-debug && cmake --build build/debug`
- [ ] `./build/debug/compiler/driver/daoc <any-file>` exits 0
- [ ] `./build/debug/compiler/driver/daoc` (no args) exits 1 with usage
message
- [ ] `clang-format --dry-run --Werror compiler/driver/main.cpp` passes
- [ ] `clang-tidy -p build/debug compiler/driver/main.cpp` reports 0
user-code warnings
- [ ] CI workflow runs green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: dao
repository.description: Dao is a modern systems and compute programming language with ML-inspired syntax, explicit execution contexts, and pipeline semantics, compiled via LLVM with a C++23 implementation

commit.message:
Add news digest talent, generic scheduler, and config-driven RSS feeds

- core/scheduler.py: new generic background scheduler that fires any
  command from settings.json "scheduler" list at configured times/days;
  fires at most once per day per entry; runs as daemon thread
- talents/news_digest.py: aggregates RSS feeds into a dated HTML file
  (YYYYMMDD.html) grouped by category (General / Technology / Infosec),
  opens in default browser; priority 55
- web_browser.py: remove hardcoded _RSS_FEEDS module dict; add
  initialize() to build self._rss_feeds from defaults + news_digest
  feeds (single source of truth) + optional web_browser.rss_feeds
  overrides in settings.json
- settings.example.json / settings.json: add news_digest block with 20
  feeds across General / Technology / Infosec categories (Reuters, BBC,
  AP, NPR, Guardian, Ars, Verge, HN, TC, Wired, Krebs, Schneier, THN,
  BleepingComputer, SANS, CISA, Naked Security, SecurityWeek, Dark
  Reading, Infosecurity Magazine); scheduler block with morning digest
  task defaulting to enabled:false
- core/assistant.py: import and start Scheduler in __init__

feat: building return — Alfred welcomes you back after 6+ hours away

New generateBuildingReturn() generator (HYPE_PROMPT, welcome mood/purple):
- Fires when a coding app becomes frontmost after 6+ hours gap
- Passes app name, hours away, project name, and last git commit for context
- One dry-but-warm sentence — not "welcome back", just lands and references where you left off
- "Six hours. The code was waiting." energy

CheckInEngine._maybeShowDistractionReturn now branches on gap size:
- >= 6h → _triggerBuildingReturn (new, 6h cooldown, welcome mood)
- >= 1h → _triggerDistractionReturn (existing, 2h cooldown, normal mood)

Distinct from:
- generateInactivityReturn (3+ day absence, days framing)
- generateDistractionReturn (browser distraction, short gap)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: CC — alert subscribers when previously-cancelled session reopens (#75)

When a session that was marked as cancelled (via /notify slot-freed) reappears
in the current schedule, push a notification to subscribers with cancelAlerts=true.
Respects cancelAlertSports filtering. Uses KV key cancelled:YYYY-WW (14-day TTL)
to track the per-week cancelled set; reopen detection runs on the */30 cron
alongside the 30-min notification handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(schematic): remove spurious symbol_instances, fix sheet_instances root path

KiCad 9 does not use a top-level (symbol_instances ...) section — ref
designators are resolved entirely via per-symbol (instances ...) blocks.
The spurious section may have confused KiCad's parser, causing "?" refs.

Also fix sheet_instances root path from "/{root_uuid}" to "/" which is
the canonical KiCad 9 format (verified against installed KiCad templates).

commit.message:
Fix: comprehensive security, performance, and DRY improvements (#1)

## Summary
- **Security**: Production config enforces env vars (no defaults), CORS
restricted, admin password from env, authorization on post endpoints,
input validation, global error handler, token revocation on logout
- **Performance**: Route-level code splitting (React.lazy), post list
returns summaries (no content), DB indexes on published/slug, eager
author loading, Nginx gzip + caching + security headers
- **DRY**: Shared project data, shared date formatter, Contact uses
api.ts, PostEditor uses own CSS classes, hardcoded colors → CSS
variables

## Deployment notes
After merging, update your `.env` on the server:
```
ADMIN_PASSWORD=<your-new-secure-password>
CORS_ORIGINS=https://chrisgonzalez.dev
```
Then run `flask seed` to update the admin password.

## Test plan
- [x] Backend: 20/20 tests passing
- [x] Frontend: TypeScript compiles with no errors
- [ ] Verify login works with new ADMIN_PASSWORD env var
- [ ] Verify CORS blocks unauthorized origins
- [ ] Verify post create/update/delete require author ownership

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Panel Door Label template with dynamic placeholders

Full-page landscape (11x8.5) label for taping inside panel doors.
Uses {OWNERSHIP_TEXT}, {EQUIPMENT_NAME}, {SERIAL_NUMBER},
{FACILITY_NAME}, {WBS_LABEL}, and {WBS} placeholders so the same
template works across different panels and customers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: RSS collector — lenient XML parsing + SSL bypass for gov feeds

Fetch raw XML via axios (with httpsAgent rejectUnauthorized:false for gov
certs), sanitize malformed XML before parsing:
- & not in entity → & (fixes CNJ, JOTA-Poder)
- attributes without value → attr="" (fixes Conjur)
- rejectUnauthorized:false fixes STF portal SSL

Previously parser.parseURL() threw on invalid XML; now uses parseString()
on cleaned payload, recovering Conjur, CNJ, TCU, JOTA-Poder and STF feeds.

tenants, clinicians, patients, appointments, assessments,
therapy_sessions, chat_messages.

Fix: remove n8n CALL_STARTED call — AI was speaking garbled greeting

English agent returns \" \" (quoted space) not bare space, causing Polly
to literally speak 'quote space quote' to callers instead of the greeting.

Hardcoded greeting now always plays — no n8n round-trip on /incoming-call.
This also removes ~500ms of latency before callers hear anything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

fix: data quality foundation — stale technicals, fundamentals, intraday (#63)

* fix: security hardening — rate limits, XSS escaping, headers

- Add rate limiting to auth endpoints (login 5/min, register 3/hr,
  change-password 5/min) via slowapi decorators
- Add security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options,
  Referrer-Policy, HSTS in production)
- Escape user-supplied data in admin_page.py and simulation_page.py
  innerHTML to prevent DOM-based XSS
- Replace leaked exception details with generic error messages in
  chat stream and briefing endpoints
- Extract shared limiter to api/rate_limit.py to avoid circular imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: data quality foundation — missing commits, fundamentals, intraday

Critical fixes:
- Add session.commit() to 5 Celery tasks that silently rolled back writes:
  technicals (single + batch), prices (single + batch), thesis matching.
  Root cause of 15-16 day stale technical indicators.

New capabilities:
- Fundamentals ingestion (ingestion/fundamentals.py): fetches PE, margins,
  FCF yield, debt/equity, ROE, growth rates from yfinance. Populates the
  previously-empty financial_metrics table. Thesis matcher can now screen
  for "Deep Value" criteria (PE < 14, FCF yield > 6%, etc.)
- Intraday bars (ingestion/intraday.py): hourly OHLCV for watchlist tickers
  only, 3x during market hours. Auto-prunes after 7 days. Budget-friendly
  (~30 tickers × 3 pulls/day via free yfinance).
- IntradayBar model + Alembic migration for intraday_bars table.
- Celery tasks + beat schedule for both new pipelines.
- 8 integration tests for fundamentals ingestion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix SQLite time.Time handling in parameterized queries

Format time.Time values as RFC3339Nano strings in appendArg when the
engine is SQLite, matching the format used by export.go. The SQLite
driver doesn't handle Go time.Time natively and produces unparseable
strings with monotonic clock components. This fixes the root cause
rather than working around it with CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add sequential signing (tenant first, then landlord) with timeline UI

Sequential signing flow:
- Tenants get signing_order=1 (email sent immediately)
- Landlords get signing_order=2 (status="waiting", no email yet)
- After all order=1 signers complete, order=2 signers automatically
  get their signing email and status changes to "pending"
- If a "waiting" signer tries their link early, they see a waiting page

TurboTenant-style vertical timeline on lease detail:
- REQUEST SENT (checkmark, timestamp)
- TENANT SIGNS (name, email, status badge, resend button)
- LANDLORD SIGNS (name, email, status badge)
- COMPLETED (download signed PDF link)
